description: RasterRelay Flux.2 Klein 9B GGUF inpainting. Seam-free colour via the measured-minimal chain: VaeDriftMatch -> ColorCalibrate -> SeamlessTone(full, seam-band) -> ReferenceColorLock.

notes.maskChannel: LoadImageMask red channel. Plugin uploads the crop-local generation/denoise mask.
notes.architecture: gen(DD) -> VAEDecode -> [scale->native] -> MaskEdgeRefine -> VaeDriftMatch(restore_unmasked) -> ColorCalibrate -> SeamlessTone(full, seam-band) -> ReferenceColorLock -> PadToDocument(change-alpha).
notes.resolution: Generation and the decode->native ImageScale (node 16) share one working resolution, so the colour/seam nodes always receive original_image and generated_image at the same size and alignment (no 1024-square vs crop-aspect mismatch).
notes.colorPipeline: VaeDriftMatch hard-restores original pixels outside the selection. ColorCalibrate inverts the model's affine colour cast. SeamlessTone diffuses the surrounding low-frequency tone into the masked region at the seam band. ReferenceColorLock forces source hue/saturation/channel-relationships on changed pixels (source_chroma_strength=1) so only generated value/structure remains - this is the load-bearing palette lock.
notes.measuredSimplification: A/B (5 colour-critical cases, scripts/run-practical-color-lock-suite-with-comfy.py) showed the previous GrainTransfer, 2nd SeamlessTone(chroma) and BackgroundPreserve passes plus the orphan refine branch added ZERO measurable benefit: out-of-mask=0, in-mask chroma/hue error=0 with or without them, seam excess equal-or-better without them. Dropping ReferenceColorLock (minimal variant) broke the palette lock (chroma error up to 259, hue up to 173 deg), so it was kept. 42 nodes -> 30.
notes.changeAlpha: PadToDocument runs with alpha_mode=change and original_image=node 10. The result PNG is transparent wherever the final crop matches the source; alpha_grow/alpha_feather stay at 0.
notes.compositeColorLock: PadToDocument precompensates RGBA against the quantized PNG alpha so Photoshop's normal alpha blend reconstructs the locked crop colour.
notes.srgb: The Photoshop panel converts the export copy to sRGB before saving the source PNG (panel.js), so ComfyUI never misreads a wide-gamut working space. For an exact round trip the working document should be sRGB.
notes.tone_radius: Gaussian sigma (px) of the tone field. Plugin sets it to ~1/8 of the smaller crop dimension.
